nodes.context: London street gang active in the interwar years, involved in bookmaking, extortion, and racecourse protection.
London gang led by Charles 'Darby' Sabini, rival of the Elephant and Castle Mob.
Birmingham-based gang led by Billy Kimber, allied with the Elephant and Castle Mob.
London gang led by George Sage, allied with the Elephant and Castle Mob.
London gang led by Freddie Gilbert, allied with the Elephant and Castle Mob.
London gang led by the McCausland brothers, conquered by the Elephant and Castle Mob.
London gang led by Alf White, conquered by the Elephant and Castle Mob.
London all-female gang led by Alice Diamond; Bert McDonald was boyfriend of Alice Diamond.
London gang listed as an ally of the Elephant and Castle Mob in the infobox.

edges.context: The Elephant and Castle Mob were rivals of the Sabini gang, battling for control of racecourse bookmaking.
The Elephant and Castle Mob were allied with the Birmingham Boys (Brummagems) led by Billy Kimber.
The Elephant and Castle Mob were allied with the Camden Town gang led by George Sage.
The Elephant and Castle Mob were allied with the Finsbury Boys led by Freddie Gilbert.
The Elephant and Castle Mob conquered the West End Boys led by the McCausland brothers.
The Elephant and Castle Mob conquered the King's Cross/Titanic gang led by Alf White.
The Hoxton Gang is listed as an ally of the Elephant and Castle Mob in the infobox.
